description: A derived node references data from another node but doesn't list it in deps

functions_bug.bug_location: checkThreshold uses closure over global threshold — implicit dependency
functions_bug.fix: Pass threshold as parameter to checkThreshold

description: Two derived nodes depend on each other, creating an infinite loop

graphspec_bug.bug_location: validate depends on format, format depends on validate — cycle
graphspec_bug.fix: Remove format from validate.deps; validate should only depend on input

functions_bug.bug_location: validate calls formatOutput which calls validate — mutual recursion
functions_bug.fix: Break the cycle: validate should not call formatOutput

description: Diamond dependency where one path is faster, causing the merge node to see stale data from the slow path

graphspec_bug.bug_location: merge node only depends on fast — missing slow in deps

functions_bug.bug_location: combine() call ignores slow result — unused variable

description: An effect node has no dependencies, so it never triggers

graphspec_bug.bug_location: log effect has empty deps — will never fire

description: A node references a function that doesn't exist in the catalog

functions_bug.bug_location: sendSlackMsg is not imported — typo for sendSlackMessage

description: A derived node expects a numeric array but its dependency produces an object with a nested array

graphspec_bug.bug_location: computeAverage expects number[] but parseResponse returns { values: number[], meta: object } — average node receives the wrapper object, not the inner array
graphspec_bug.fix: Add an extraction node between parse and average, or change computeAverage to unwrap .values

functions_bug.bug_location: parsed is an object with .values, not an array — .reduce() will throw TypeError

description: Two async producer nodes feed a merge node, but one is not awaited, so the merge fires before both paths complete

graphspec_bug.bug_location: render depends only on fetchProfile — it fires before fetchOrders resolves, so order data is missing from the page
graphspec_bug.fix: Add 'fetchOrders' to render.deps so the graph waits for both

functions_bug.bug_location: ordersPromise is created but never awaited — buildPage receives undefined for orders
functions_bug.fix: const [profile, orders] = await Promise.all([profilePromise, ordersPromise]); return buildPage(profile, orders);

description: A fan-out/fan-in topology causes the same event to be processed twice when both branches merge

graphspec_bug.bug_location: total depends on orders directly AND through tax/shipping — the order amount gets added once from the raw order and again via tax/shipping, doubling the base price
graphspec_bug.fix: Remove 'orders' from total.deps; total should only combine tax and shipping (which already include the base amount)

functions_bug.bug_location: order.amount is counted twice — once in the raw addition and once inside calcTax/calcShipping which return amount+tax and amount+shipping respectively
functions_bug.fix: const total = tax + shipping - order.amount; or restructure so tax/shipping return only the delta

description: An async producer can reject, but no error-handling node catches it, causing the entire pipeline to silently stall

graphspec_bug.bug_location: No error handler on feed — if rssFeed returns a network error, parse and display never fire and no fallback is shown
graphspec_bug.fix: Add an error-handler node: { type: 'derived', deps: ['feed'], fn: 'handleFeedError', onError: true } that provides a fallback value, and make parse depend on the error-handler output

functions_bug.bug_location: No error handling around fetch or parseArticles — a network error or malformed XML causes an unhandled rejection
functions_bug.fix: Wrap in try/catch, render an error state on failure: try { ... } catch (e) { renderFeed([]); logError(e); }

description: A pipeline merges header and body in the wrong order, producing malformed output

graphspec_bug.bug_location: assemble lists deps as [buildBody, buildHeader] — concatParts receives body first, header second, producing body-then-header output
graphspec_bug.fix: Change assemble.deps to ['buildHeader', 'buildBody'] so concatParts receives header first

functions_bug.bug_location: concatParts called with (body, header) instead of (header, body) — response starts with body content

description: A caching node returns an expired value because it doesn't check the timestamp of the cached entry

graphspec_bug.bug_location: cacheOrFetch checks if the key exists in cache but never checks the entry's age — returns results cached hours ago when the underlying data has changed
graphspec_bug.fix: Add TTL check in cacheOrFetch: if (cache[key] && (now - cache[key].ts) < TTL) return cache[key].value; else fetch fresh

functions_bug.bug_location: cache.has(query) returns true for entries cached arbitrarily long ago — no TTL or invalidation
functions_bug.fix: Store { value, timestamp } and check if Date.now() - entry.timestamp < MAX_AGE before returning cached result

description: A derived node writes back to its own dependency, creating an infinite re-evaluation loop

graphspec_bug.bug_location: increment reads counter, adds 1, and writes back to counter — counter changes, re-triggering increment, looping forever
graphspec_bug.fix: Use a separate trigger node (e.g. a button or timer producer) to control when increment fires, rather than letting it self-trigger through counter

functions_bug.bug_location: The change listener calls setCounter which fires all listeners including itself — infinite recursion
functions_bug.fix: Guard the listener: only increment when triggered by an external event, not by its own previous write

description: A billing node receives customer and product data in the wrong parameter positions because deps are listed in the wrong order

graphspec_bug.bug_location: invoice.deps lists [product, customer] but createInvoice(customer, product) expects customer first — customer gets the product object and vice versa
graphspec_bug.fix: Change invoice.deps to ['customer', 'product'] to match createInvoice's parameter order

functions_bug.bug_location: createInvoice receives productData as customer and customerData as product — billTo will be the product title, item will be undefined

description: A pipeline starts from a derived node that has no upstream producer, so it never receives initial data

graphspec_bug.bug_location: normalize depends on 'rawData' but no node named rawData exists — the entire pipeline is inert because the source is never defined
graphspec_bug.fix: Add a producer node: rawData: { type: 'producer', source: 'inputStream' }

functions_bug.bug_location: rawData is used but never declared — throws ReferenceError at runtime
functions_bug.fix: Add data source: const rawData = await readInputStream(); before the pipeline

description: A producer node references a misspelled configuration field, causing it to connect to the wrong endpoint

graphspec_bug.bug_location: params.urlField is 'apiURL' (capital RL) but config.initial has 'apiUrl' (lowercase rl) — fetchData reads config['apiURL'] which is undefined, falling back to a default/wrong URL
graphspec_bug.fix: Change params.urlField to 'apiUrl' to match the config key

functions_bug.bug_location: config.apiURL is undefined (should be config.apiUrl) — fetch receives undefined as URL, throws TypeError or hits wrong endpoint
